item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author, this was your first SCP? I know I'm impressed.

oh yeah upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this for all the reasons already well-expressed above.
Also the flipped idea that to a lovecraftian demigod a mundane (if tragic) sequence of human events is unfathomable. So IT treats the accident like a low-level shaman with an heirloom summoning ritual. IT doesn't know why or how this little dance works, only that going through the motions brings what IT wants . So try very hard not to screw up the ritual too much!

But that final interview is pretty much the essence of deus ex machina. That information needs to be in the article for it to work so well, but there is not even a hint of fridge logic for how the passengers know what they know.

It's enough to take me from an enthusiastic upvote to a tentative one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everything in this has character;

  • The SCP itself is very interesting and unique, giving it, "character"
  • The D-class and doctor in the interview also have some personality. And it's pretty hard to give the scientists some personality in the interviews correctly, since they're supposed to be so formal.
  • The entity also has some character, which is shown in the interview.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was somewhat indifferent to this one until the last line, but I love the idea that the entity - whatever it is - isn't smart enough to realize that it could have more than one head if it just influences everybody in the car or anyone even near the car. And then I upvoted. I love this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact anqxyr or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I missed the part about being able to see the world as it was from the inside out when there wasn't a driver. Good show. Literally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I should point out that we can also find out a lot of details just by getting people to sit in the back seat when the car has no driver.

Once we've figured out who the woman was and where her city was, we can check on the schedules of local schools, and compare this to when we see local children run by the car, etc — and that doesn't require sacrificing anyone to the thing that likes heads. (Unless someone touches the driver's seat, of course.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Voct, I think I'm in love with your slice-of-time SCPs. They're peppered with just enough minor details to piece together a loosely guided cause of narrative.

I'm actually most impressed with the fact that enough people were pushed through this thing - and subsequently beheaded - to gather exactly who this woman is, the exact date, the names of various establishments, the exact times, and the schedules of local schools.

Reminds me of the ending of SCP-374, the … wait a minute… you wrote that one too!?!

Sweet mother of 343…

+1

item.title: Re: a discussion worth saving
item.content:encoded: This just added a whole new layer of awesome to this scp.

item.title: Re: a discussion worth saving
item.content:encoded: I'd like this better in a collapsible… :/

item.title: a discussion worth saving
item.content:encoded:
+ IRC discussion
- well, /I/ thought it was interesting

<Freudian> so any time someone's head goes through the windshield
<Freudian> it takes it
<Freudian> that's wonderful.
<Voct> and we don't know WHY it takes the heads
<Voct> I was telling this to Vehemency
<Voct> V's response:
<Voct> NEW FRIENDS YAY!
<Freudian> lol
<Freudian> that's wonderful
<Freudian> I love it
<Voct> an interdimensional horror that's *superstitious*
<Freudian> it's powerful enough to fully recreate 1996 for this woman
<Voct> well, not *fully*
<Freudian> enough of it to work
<Voct> if you open the door and get out of the car, you're back in the present
<Voct> she can't get out of the car
<Voct> she just *thinks* that she gets out of the car
<Freudian> true
<Voct> that's why she goes catatonic
<Freudian> well "she"
<Voct> we don't know if it *could* recreate 1996 fully for this woman
<Voct> oh, one detail I'm really pleased with
<Voct> and that no one notices
<Voct> is where the accident took place
<Freudian> outside [redacted] labs?
<Freudian> is that
<Freudian> Prometheus
Voct shrugs
<Voct> maybe
<Voct> and it was a *collision with a delivery van*
<Voct> what was being delivered?
<Voct> does it matter?
<Freudian> heh
<Voct> answer: MAYBE
<Voct> WE DON'T KNOW
<Freudian> I like the idea that in the Foundationverse this is the sort of thing that can happen
<Voct> and we're damn well not going to try it again
<Voct> yeah
<Voct> that exact car accident
<Voct> *that* head, hitting *that* windshield, at *that* place and time
<Voct> triggered a dimensional rupture
<Freudian> you can be driving down the road and suddenly your head goes through an interdimensional rift to become one of the dollies at a monster's teaparty
<Freudian> that is my canon for it
<Voct> well, it *was* on the road
<Voct> but the monster thought that the rift was situated relative to the car's windshield
<Voct> so now it is
<Voct> the D-class wasn't sure about the word "eat"
<Voct> it does…. *something* with them
<Freudian> who are we to say what it does with them
<Freudian> who are we to try and conceive of what this thing can do or want
<Voct> well
<Voct> we know what it *wants*
<Voct> it wants human heads!
<Freudian> true
<Freudian> what do you see, when you picture the thing on the other side?
<Voct> a dark cloud
<Voct> darker than a cloud should be
<Voct> with tentacles reaching
<Voct> and lightning
<Freudian> I see the blackness of space, stretching out as far as the eye can see.
<Freudian> And then a patch slightly darker. I can't tell how big it is.
<Voct> yeah
<Freudian> Or where its boundaries are.
<Voct> darker than it should be
<Voct> and it's fuzzy like a cloud
<Freudian> yes
<Freudian> yeah
<Freudian> but I can tell that it's thinking
<Freudian> and it's looking through the windshield
<Voct> and it's waiting
<Freudian> and maybe
<Freudian> we shouldn't be giving it so many brains
<Freudian> because if it can use them
<Voct> …………….. maybe.
<Voct> that's something, yes. Maybe when the D-class said that "eating" wasn't the right word
<Freudian> the question has changed in my head
<Voct> well, it's not *consuming* them
<Voct> but it's *using* them
<Voct> using them up?
<Freudian> from "why does it want them" to "what does it want them for"
<Voct> integrating them into its own form?
<Voct> if it can do all this to human brains….
<Freudian> if a Foundation researcher got into the front seat
<Freudian> I can't imagine how bad that would be
<Voct> well
<Voct> that's one thing you can relax about
<Voct> because it always overwrites everything in its victim's brain
<Freudian> Yep
<Freudian> oh shit right
<Freudian> it doesn't have any minds
<Freudian> other than hers
<Voct> it does this because it's not smart
<Voct> if it was just a little bit smarter, it could *become* smarter
<Freudian> you mean
<Freudian> if it didn't overwrite
<Voct> Yeah
<Voct> instead it's just getting more and more copies of her head
<Freudian> thank GOD for superstition
<Freudian> …no pun intended
<Voct> heh
<Voct> on the other hand, quantity does have a quality all its own….
<Freudian> parallel processors
<Freudian> two heads are better tha-
Freudian is shot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting re-write, but as written it's confusing and the final interview feels like a comedian explaining the joke to an audience that wasn't laughing. Neutral for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is drastically improved from the original version. I like the interview log at the end. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So any attempt at packing the backseat would then result in no driving occurring and the driver insisting on their removal, then. Okay!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not without the driver's consent. And this driver won't take more than 3 passengers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If they're relative to local time, then the times should probably be written in military format.

And it's not unusual for more people to climb into a car than is good, especially if the back seat is a bench. See also: teenagers, college students. What about people hanging out in the trunk?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The asterisk was a glitch. Removed.

And the reason that there can only be one driver and three passengers is that there's only four seats in the car.

The times are relative to where the car is. I considered commenting on how it reacts to Daylight Savings, but couldn't decide how it reacts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would those times be relative to the car's internal clock-radio? Should make that clear. What happens when a fourth person tries to enter the car? And is the asterisk in the interview log intentional? And you can probably leave the expletives in.

Otherwise, rewritten like a boss. Good work, Voct!

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
A very happy birthday, it looks like.

Deletion vote cancelled.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: …Not quite that bad, but yeah.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Thank you! :)

item.title: Just my two cents
item.content:encoded: Hey Voct, so I love what you did with it, it's a great concept and read, but I wouldn't be doushy ol' me if I didn't put my two cents in. I love the creature and the effect it has on the subjects and everything you did but I just want to say that this was completely off from what I originally conceived. Maybe a possessed car isn't good enough to be a SCP, perhaps perhaps, but the entire point of the original was that she was possessing them and they relieved her death, not something waiting on the other side. Great idea, just got a little irked by how far off the original it was. Anyways, loved it.

item.title: Re: Thoughts
item.content:encoded:
Um.. that's kinda the edge the item has. Otherwise it'd be a boring anomalous car.

This way.. asides from the odd behaviour (and who knows it's human? I think anything that's able to reason might be prone to superstition) it's somewhat worrying - what if one day, it does get smarter?

All in all ,this is an excellent rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bravo, Voct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very pleased to take this out of the negatives, nice work Voct, in my opinion you nailed it.

Edit: Just had an idea. If this thing could do far more, and far worse, if it strayed from its original conditions, might it be that it's in the Foundation's best interests to make sure that it doesn't do so?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not saying it was, just saying it *felt* a bit that way as I read it. Simply my two cents, and not enough to take away the upvote from me.

item.title: Re: Thoughts
item.content:encoded: Oh. Hey. Now it's better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed the typo.

The entity was not a last-minute add-on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's just two things with this that I noticed post-rewrite. First is an apparent typo: "The environment persists until 9:04 PM — the time at which SCP-1061 was in its collision; after 6:04 PM," Which one was it, 9 or 6?

Second, the aspect of the "entity" causing is just seems kind of… Added on. I mean it's a decent hook IMO, but it literally has the look that you got done typing, thought it needed a bit extra, and just put the last paragraph and interview in there with no lead-up or anything. Maybe expose a bit more behind the [DATA EXPUNGED] at the point of impact, indicating some outside force beyond just the semi-illusionary crash.

item.title: Re: Thoughts
item.content:encoded: The only part of this that isn't awesome for me is the idea of a superstitious pandimensional horror. It humanises them, I like the idea of the heads disappearing on going through the windscreen inexplicably.

item.title: Re: Thoughts
item.content:encoded: …Well this sure is interesting.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
I'm not saying it was Bobble.

But it was Bobble.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: You know what would explain the rest of it? If the clowns were ancient aliens.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: … This explains. So so much.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Happy birthday!

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
You hear that Voct? You BETTER make a good rewrite, or you are going to RUIN little Camerons birthday! Do you really want to be that guy? You'd be just as bad as the clown at my eighth birthday party, who could only make one kind of balloon animal, and it was phallic, and went in your butt, but you couldn't tell anyone, because then the clown would have all his buddies spirit you away in the night, and they'd stuff you in the trunk of their little clown car, and ALL of them would show you their SPECIAL balloon animals, and you'd think greasepaint would be easy to get off, but no, it stayed on my thighs for weeks, but my daddy never noticed, he was too busy with that whore he called a secretary and when I tried to tell mom, she just poured another shot, and the clown kept coming back, and he brought friends, do you have any idea what it's like to be a whore for so many clowns?

Ahem. Don't fuck this up voct.

item.title: Thoughts
item.content:encoded:
Okay, yeah, I'm gonna do a full rewrite on this one.

The basic ideas I see here… there's essentially three anomalous phenomena manifesting in this one car. One is linked to the windows (the illusion of an intact car from 1996, and of the surrounding neighborhood), one is linked to the driver's seat (if you're in the driver's seat, your mind gets overwritten with that of the last driver), and one is linked to the windshield (the driver's head always goes towards the same place on the windshield, and then…?)

(The related log file is going to have to go, by the way. It won't work with the new version.)

We have to keep the windshield boarded up except during experiments, by the way. And what happens if your head touches the windshield: if you're sitting in the driver's seat and have been overwritten, then your head just… disappears. This always leads to huge spouting gushers of blood and spinal fluid. None of the heads have been recovered, although when we planted a transmitter in someone's skull, we got signal for a few minutes after disappearance. But when we tried that a second time, the signal just cut off instantly.

If you're sitting in the passenger seat and your head touches the windshield — which you have to do deliberately, you're not re-enacting the accident because you're not in the driver's seat — then you pass out. When you wake up, you suffer from constant nausea and seizures — the side effects of bad concussion, without actually the main symptoms of head injury.

There is an entity which wants human heads. It's hugely powerful, but not very smart. The first time it got a human head was in 1996 (the impact of that head on that windshield at that exact moment opened up a dimensional portal. Less likely things have happened in the SCP-verse!).

The entity isn't quite sure what part of the incident led to the driver's head hitting the windshield, but it knows that it likes human heads. So it's replicating as many of the circumstances from that first time as it can. You're sitting in the car? Then everything you see through the windows must look EXACTLY like it did back in 1996. You turn on the radio? Then the radio will play EXACTLY what was being broadcast in 1996. And if you sit in the driver's seat, then you must have EXACTLY the same mind as the driver did back in 1996.

If the entity was smarter, it would be influencing everyone who touches the car: they'd be lining up for a chance to stick their heads in the hole and be eaten. Hell, it could influence everyone who so much as sees the car.

But it's not smart. It's superstitious, and it's not willing to mess with a good thing.

And it likes heads.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: That's my birthday :(

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This page will be deleted on Saturday, April 21st. You've got until then.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Fourthed.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: that depends on whether you're going to go ahead and delete it before I'm finished.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: I was under the impression you were merely "polishing," which shouldn't take as long, I don't think. Are you actually doing a complete rewrite of this one?

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Wait wait, I'm in the middle of rewriting it?

May I have a bit of an extension?

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Second.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Seconding.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Suggesting deletion at -9.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think what bothers me about this is that it violates one of the fundamental rules of writing, "Show, don't tell."
Perhaps that is why I find it uninteresting.
I don't think it's bad, really. Just… boring?

item.title: Re: Detailed Disassembly
item.content:encoded:
Agreeing wholeheartedly with this.

What particularly struck me was the D-class going through the windshield during the test and disappearing. There's a lot of good concepts and atmosphere here, just needs work on the writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [[SCP-704]] was the first thing that came to mind, but it's also pretty different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For your reference on future SCPs (and I think this same shorthand is in one of the documents somewhere):

Safe - lock it in a room, leave it alone, nothing will happen. (A nuclear weapon with no arming device is Safe.)

Euclid - lock it in a room, leave it alone, something might or might not happen. (A nuclear weapon with a remote arming device, when we don't have the remote detonator, is Euclid.)

Keter - lock it in a room, leave it alone, and we're all gonna die. (An armed nuclear weapon, counting down to detonation, is Keter.)

Oh, and no vote yet - this one doesn't grab me as such, but I can see potential there…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: not if it goes past like, -15 i think

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, please do. Much appreciated

item.title: Detailed Disassembly
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so, this is your first SCP, and it's received upvotes… but more downvotes.
Here's what I can note:

The idea that you're trying to get forward is very intriguing.
However, the execution was rather poor.

Your writing style could really use some work - it's not bad for a start, but a lot of polish is needed.

All in all, I have not voted on this one, since it's not bad, but could use some work.
Create a sandbox, and test out your works there.
Also, drop by in site19, and we can always review your ideas and such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I rather like the idea, and so have upvoted. It needs a lot of polishing, though. May I?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is good but the writing isn't quite there. Will upvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the clarification of euclid, I suppose I didn't fully grasp the concept. Changes made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How, precisely, is this supposed to be a Euclid? I mean, there's nothing that forces someone to do the stupid thing and get into the car, and even that could still be considered safe (provided definite limits to the area and it being a passive effect). Euclid means that the SCP has an inherent element of unpredictability and/or uncertainty. Putting it another way, a safe SCP is something you can build a structure around, lock the structure up, and then safely forget it exists until you need it, and this SCP currently reads just like that.

Beyond that, there's a minor formatting error in the containment procedures; or at least I see it as such: when articles describe the dimensions of an area, the multiplication operator is not capitalized. Minor, yeah, but it sort of throws the style off a bit.

Downvoting for the above reasons, though if improvements are made, I'd be happy to remove that vote or change it to an upvote depending on magnitude of improvements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's alright, but it needs polish.

Being your first, withholding vote. I'm currently working on my first as well. I'd advise you to use the sandbox, and the realtime chat before posting in the future, but that's just me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd be more interesting if there were some signs that the person was aware of something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This could actually be a good SCP if it's cleaned up a bit. Vote withheld.
